title: Critique of Pure Reason
author: Immanuel Kant
content: “Thoughts without content are empty, intuitions without concepts are blind.”
action: Pair raw data with structured frameworks to ensure usability.

title: Being and Nothingness
author: Jean-Paul Sartre
content: “Man is condemned to be free; because once thrown into the world, he is responsible for everything he does.”
action: Accept total accountability for all actions taken with your agency.

title: The Prince
author: Niccolò Machiavelli
content: “The first method for estimating the intelligence of a ruler is to look at the men he has around him.”
action: Evaluate the caliber of your associates as a mirror of your own competence.

title: Tao Te Ching
author: Lao Tzu
content: “He who knows others is wise; he who knows himself is enlightened.”
action: Balance the study of others with deep self-calibration.

title: Fear and Trembling
author: Søren Kierkegaard
content: “Life is not a problem to be solved, but a reality to be experienced.”
action: Recognize the limits of analytical problem-solving in subjective experience.
